title: Other researchers, unspecified discipline
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 51 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct research in a particular field of knowledge and publish findings in professional journals, books, or electronic media.
Keep abreast of developments in the field by reading current literature, talking with colleagues, and participating in professional conferences.
Plan, evaluate, and revise curricula, course content, course materials, and methods of instruction.
Advise students on academic and vocational curricula and on career issues.

Prepare and deliver lectures to undergraduate or graduate students on various topics.
Select and obtain materials and supplies, such as textbooks and laboratory equipment.
Supervise students' laboratory work and field work; coordinate laboratory operations.

Perform administrative duties, such as serving as department head; hire new faculty; supervise undergraduate or graduate teaching, internship, and research work; and direct research of other teachers or graduate students working for advanced academic degrees.
Maintain student attendance records, grades, and other required records.
Collaborate with members of the business community to improve programs, develop new programs, and provide student access to learning opportunities, such as internships.
Perform administrative duties, including serving as department head.
Initiate, facilitate, and moderate classroom discussions.
Maintain geographic information systems laboratories, performing duties such as updating software.
Perform spatial analysis and modeling using geographic information system techniques.
Evaluate and grade students' class work, laboratory work, assignments, and papers, and maintain required records.
Maintain regularly scheduled office hours to advise and assist students and provide guidance on academic and vocational curricula and career issues.
Supervise undergraduate or graduate teaching, internship, and research work, as well as laboratory or field work.
Collaborate with colleagues to address teaching and research issues, and serve on academic or administrative committees.
Investigate diseases or parasites to determine cause and risk factors, progress, life cycle, or mode of transmission.
Plan, administer, and evaluate health safety standards and programs to improve public health.
Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical application, analyzing data and presenting findings.
Consult with and advise various professionals regarding medical applications of sciences.
Supervise professional, technical, and clerical personnel.
Oversee public health programs and conduct statistical analysis.
Identify and analyze public health issues related to foodborne parasitic diseases.
Inform and respond to public regarding wildlife and conservation issues.
Research and control animal selection and breeding practices to increase production efficiency and improve animal quality.
Develop new food items for production, based on consumer feedback and ensure compliance with environmental laws.
Stay up to date on new regulations and current events regarding food science by reviewing scientific literature.
Prepare technical and research reports and communicate the results to relevant stakeholders.
Review reports and proposals for accuracy and adherence to policies.
Inventory or estimate plant and wildlife populations, and supervise biological technicians and technologists as well as other scientists in related research.

Develop pest management and control measures and conduct risk assessments related to pest exclusion, using scientific methods, and plan and administer biological research programs for government, research firms, medical industries, or manufacturing firms.
Study reactions of plants, animals, and marine species to parasites and study aquatic plants and animals and environmental conditions affecting them, such as pollution.

Develop methods and apparatus for securing representative plant, animal, aquatic, or soil samples; conduct research concerning animal nutrition, breeding, or management to improve products or processes.
Prepare plans for management of renewable resources and conduct literature reviews.

Study basic principles of plant and animal life, such as origin, relationship, development, anatomy, and function; and study characteristics of animals, including origin, interrelationships, classification, life histories, diseases, development, genetics, and distribution.
Teach or supervise students and perform research at universities and colleges, and instruct undergraduate and graduate students within the areas of cellular or molecular biology.
Develop improved practices in feeding, housing, sanitation, or parasite and disease control of animals and conduct research to develop methodologies and procedures for medical applications.

Collect and analyze biological data about relationships among and between organisms and their environment, and investigate the relationship between organisms and disease, including the control of epidemics.

Prepare or review reports, manuscripts, or meeting presentations, including writing grant proposals to obtain funding for research, and disseminate research findings through articles or presentations.
Analyze characteristics of animals to identify and classify them, and examine physiological, morphological, and cultural characteristics using microscopes to identify and classify microorganisms.
Write grant applications to obtain funding and grant proposals to support biological and epidemiological research.
Monitor and perform tests on water, food, and the environment to detect harmful microorganisms or obtain information about sources of pollution.

Study effects of management practices, processing methods, feed, or environmental conditions on quality and quantity of animal products, and evaluate food processing and storage operations to assist in developing quality assurance programs.
Evaluate effects of drugs, gases, pesticides, parasites, and microorganisms at various levels and research how characteristics of plants or animals are carried through successive generations.
Develop new or improved ways of preserving, processing, packaging, storing, and delivering foods, and study the chemistry of living processes in relation to animal and plant health.
Investigation and evaluation of new technologies to enhance agricultural practices and analyze large datasets for research purposes.
Create novel computational approaches and analytical tools as required by research goals, including design and application of bioinformatics algorithms such as machine learning.
Develop food standards and production specifications as well as safety and sanitary regulations.

Conduct research on cell organization and function, including mechanisms of gene expression and cellular bioinformatics; and coordinate molecular or cellular research activities with scientists specializing in other fields.
Supervise technical personnel and postdoctoral research fellows, and monitor and report incidents of infectious diseases to local and state health agencies.
Study methods to improve aspects of foods, such as chemical composition and nutritional value.
Investigate the nature, composition, or expression of genes, including research into genetic engineering.
Confer with health departments, industry personnel, physicians, and others to develop health safety standards and public health improvement programs.
Participate in all levels of bioproduct development, including proposing new products and performing market analyses.
Provide statistical and computational tools for biologically based activities, such as genetic analysis, measurement of gene expression, or gene function determination.
Instruct others in the selection and use of bioinformatics tools and improve user interfaces to bioinformatics software and databases.

Communicate research findings on various types of diseases to health practitioners, policy makers, and the public. Consult with engineers or other technical personnel working on construction projects about the effects of soil problems and possible solutions to these problems. Communicate research or project results to other professionals or the public or teach related courses, seminars, or workshops.
Compile data for use in activities, such as gene expression profiling, genome annotation, or structural bioinformatics.

Study ways to improve agricultural sustainability, such as the use of new methods of composting. Investigate responses of soils to specific management practices to determine the use capabilities of soils and the effects of alternative practices on soil productivity.

Identify or classify species of insects or allied forms, such as mites or spiders. Study insect distribution or habitat and recommend methods to prevent importation or spread of injurious species. Conduct experiments regarding causes of bee diseases or factors affecting yields of nectar or pollen.

Develop environmentally safe methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or pests. Develop new or improved methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or insect pests.

Conduct experiments to investigate the underlying mechanisms of plant growth and response to the environment. Conduct experiments to develop new or improved varieties of field crops, focusing on characteristics such as yield, quality, disease resistance, nutritional value, or adaptation to specific soils or climates. Conduct experiments investigating how soil forms, changes, or interacts with land-based ecosystems or living organisms.
Research technical requirements or environmental impacts of urban green spaces, such as green roof installations.
Conduct research into the use of plant species as green fuels or in the production of green fuels.
Plan or supervise waste management programs for composting or farming. Plan or supervise land conservation or reclamation programs for industrial development projects.

Develop ways of altering soils to suit different types of plants. Develop improved measurement techniques, soil conservation methods, soil sampling devices, or related technology. Study soil characteristics to classify soils on the basis of factors such as geographic location, landscape position, or soil properties. Identify degraded or contaminated soils and develop plans to improve their chemical, biological, or physical characteristics.

Provide advice regarding the development of regulatory standards for land reclamation or soil conservation. Provide information or recommendations to farmers or other landowners regarding ways in which they can best use land, promote plant growth, or avoid or correct problems such as erosion.
Investigate soil problems or poor water quality to determine sources and effects.
Survey undisturbed or disturbed lands for classification, inventory, mapping, environmental impact assessments, environmental protection planning, conservation planning, or reclamation planning.

emerging_tasks.task: Write letters of recommendation for students.
Correspond with students through email to address their questions and concerns.
Prepare and deliver lectures to undergraduate or graduate students on topics such as structural geology, micrometeorology, atmospheric thermodynamics, and physics.
Review articles to determine their suitability for publication.
Perform tests on water, food, and the environment to detect harmful microorganisms or to obtain information about sources of pollution, contamination, or infection and interpret the results.
Teach epidemiology to students in public health programs.
Test processing equipment to ensure products are produced according to specifications.
Use advanced technologies, such as GIS, remote sensing, and drone technology, for wildlife tracking, habitat mapping, and population studies.
